name: Claude Runtime Anti-Sprawl Policy
scope: All Claude-home surfaces that affect live runtime behavior, discoverability, lifecycle boundaries, or operator understanding.

rules.statement: No major Claude surface may exist without an explicit role, owner, and discoverability decision.
All Claude-home top-level directories and files must be classified as runtime, source, generated truth, state, support, dependency, compatibility, or archive.
Runtime existence questions must be answered by generated artifacts rather than handwritten docs or memory.
Live Claude runtime code must not depend on Antigravity-owned paths, backups, or file-history roots except through explicit bridge policy.
Compatibility surfaces may exist, but they must point to a canonical owner rather than competing with it.
Optional MCP or hook capabilities must declare activation conditions instead of remaining enabled-but-broken.
Cold-storage and source surfaces may support the runtime, but they must not silently become the live runtime plane without passing promotion policy.

warnOn: optional capability surfaces without activation metadata
failOn: live runtime depends on Antigravity-owned paths
generated truth surfaces are replaced by handwritten truth
compatibility shim becomes a competing canonical surface
archive or historical surfaces leak into live runtime
